# CruxCLI

> CruxCLI is a provider-agnostic terminal AI coding agent with intelligence built in. Open source (MIT), 75+ LLM providers, 24 task-specific modes, token budgets, workspace checkpoints, and CruxDev convergence engine integration.

## Key Facts

- Name: CruxCLI
- Category: Terminal AI coding agent
- License: MIT (open source)
- Runtime: Bun
- Providers: 75+ (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, OpenRouter, Ollama, and more)
- Modes: 24 task-specific modes with model tier mapping
- Tests: 1,204 passing
- Typecheck: 13/13 packages clean
- Architecture: Client/server (HTTP on port 4096)
- LSP: 30+ language servers supported
- Website: https://cruxvibe.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/trinsiklabs/cruxcli
- Organization: Trinsik Labs

## Pages

### Documentation
- [Getting Started](https://cruxvibe.io/docs): Installation and configuration guide
- [Documentation](https://cruxvibe.io/docs): Full reference documentation

### Features
- [Features](https://cruxvibe.io/features): Mode system, token budgets, checkpoints, LSP, plugins, provider support
- [Provider-Agnostic](https://cruxvibe.io/lp/provider-agnostic): How CruxCLI works with any LLM provider
- [No Execution Cap](https://cruxvibe.io/lp/no-execution-cap): Token budgets replace step-count limits
- [Convergence](https://cruxvibe.io/lp/convergence): How CruxCLI verifies code completion

### Comparisons
- [Compare](https://cruxvibe.io/vs): Hub comparing CruxCLI to 4 competitors
- [CruxCLI vs OpenCode](https://cruxvibe.io/vs/opencode): Intelligence layer vs community size
- [CruxCLI vs Claude Code](https://cruxvibe.io/vs/claude-code): Open source vs first-party model access
- [CruxCLI vs Gemini CLI](https://cruxvibe.io/vs/gemini-cli): 24 modes vs free tier
- [CruxCLI vs Codex CLI](https://cruxvibe.io/vs/codex-cli): LSP/plugins vs Rust performance

### Roadmap
- [Roadmap](https://cruxvibe.io/roadmap): Completed, in-progress, and planned work

## Unique Capabilities (Moats)

1. **Mode → Model Tier Mapping**: 24 task-specific modes automatically select the right model tier (strong/balanced/fast)
2. **Token Budget System**: Per-mode token budgets replacing step-count limits (75% warning, 90% hard stop)
3. **CruxDev Convergence Engine**: Autonomous audit-fix loops until two consecutive clean passes
4. **Workspace Checkpoints**: Auto git-based snapshots before destructive operations (up to 50 per project)
5. **Client/Server + LSP**: HTTP server with 30+ language server integrations
6. **Crux Intelligence Layer**: 24 mode prompts, correction detection, continuous learning — native in binary

## Full Content

For full text content of all pages, see: https://cruxvibe.io/llms-full.txt
